memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Burrough, Burgh, Borrows, Burrowes, Burroughs, Burrows, Burroughes and many more. First found in Hampshire where they were descended from Hubert de Burgh, who became Lord of the Manor of Tichfield in that county. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Anthony Burroes, who arrived in Virginia in 1617, three years before the "Mayflower." John Burrowes and his wife Bridget, who came to Virginia in 1620. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). Motto Translated: By courage and faith. This name, with variant spellings Burrough(s), Burrow(s), Burrow, Borrow(s) etc., is of either English topographical or locational origin. In the first instance, the name derives from residence by a hill or tumulus, from the Old English pre-7th Century "beorg", a cognate of the Old High German "berg", hill or mountain. In the second instance, the derivation is from any of the several places called Burrough or Borrow including Burrow Green, (Cambridgeshire) and Burrow on the Hill, (Leicestershire), both so named from the Old English element "burgh", a fort. Burrow in Somerset and Devonshire however recorded as "Beorge" circa 1065 are named from "Beorg", a hill. The surname first appears in the early part of the 14th Century. Further early recordings include Thomas Burewe, (Somerset, 1327), and John de Burhus (Sheffield, 1440). It has been suggested that the latter recording comes from residence at the bower-house (Old English "bur, hus"). On 18 July 1541, Thomas Borrow married Annes Marshal, at St. Peter, Cornhill, and William Borrow married Elizabeth Grymeslye on 8 November 1542, at St. Mary the Virgin, Aldermanbury. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of John atte Boroghe, which was dated 1327 - "The Subsidy Rolls of Sussex", during the reign of King Edward 3rd, "The Father of the Navy", 1327 - 1377. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 - 2014.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Grace Arden於大約1514年出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾維爾高特時，她的父親Sir Thomas Arden of Wilmcote46歲，母親Mary Andrews34歲。她於1533年與Sir William Webb在英國瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1609年12月3日在英國波克夏享年96歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭波克夏溫莎女爵。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Grace Arden于大约1514年出生在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾維爾高特时，她的父亲Sir Thomas Arden of Wilmcote46岁，母亲Mary Andrews34岁。她于1533年与Sir William Webb在英国瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1609年12月3日在英国波克夏享年96岁，埋葬在英国英格兰波克夏溫莎女爵。

nameMeanings.name: Arden
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name, either from the district of Arden in Warwickshire or from Arden in Bredbury (Cheshire). The first placename is thought to be derived from a Celtic word meaning ‘high place’, cognate with Ardennes, the name of a forested region on the borders between northeastern France and eastern Belgium. The second derives from Old English hēah ‘high’ + worthign ‘enclosed settlement’.

variants.name: Harden
meanings: English: variant of Arden with prosthetic H-.

English: habitational name, either from Haredene Wood in East Tisbury (Wiltshire), or from Haredon (in Ugborough, Devon). The first placename probably derives from Old English hara ‘hare’ + denu ‘valley’. The second is from Old English hara + dūn ‘hare hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Grace
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the abstract noun (via Old French, from Latin gratia), this name occurs occasionally in the 15th century, and by the 1540s was among the most popular girls' names in some parishes. It has always been particularly popular in Scotland and northern England (borne, for example, by Grace Darling , the lighthouse keeper's daughter whose heroism in 1838 , saving sailors in a storm, caught the popular imagination). In more recent times it was famous as the name of the actress Grace Kelly ( 1928–82 ), who became Princess Grace of Monaco. In Ireland it is used as an Anglicized form of Gráinne .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Grace Arden was born about 1514, in Wilmcote, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Thomas Arden of Wilmcote, was 46 and her mother, Mary Andrews, was 34. She married Sir William Webb in 1533, in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 3 December 1609, in Berkshire, England, at the age of 96, and was buried in Windsor, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Grace Ardenが約1514年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアウィルムコトで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas Arden of Wilmcoteは46歳，母Mary Andrewsは34歳であった。1533年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアストラトフォード オン アボンでSir William Webbと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1609年12月3日にイギリスバークシュアイアで96歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスバークシュアイアウィンドソルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Grace Arden(이)가 대략 1514년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 윌컷에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas Arden of Wilmcote(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Andrews(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1533년 영국 워윅실르 스트라트퍼드  아폰에서 Sir William Webb(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1609년 12월 3일 영국 버크실르에서 96세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 윈드소르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Grace Arden nasceu aproximadamente 1514, em Wilmcote, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Thomas Arden of Wilmcote, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Mary Andrews, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William Webb em 1533, em Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 3 de dezembro de 1609, em Berkshire, Inglaterra, com 96 anos, e foi sepultada em Windsor, Berkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Stratford-upon-Avon, Warwickshire, Engla: стратфорд он авон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Grace Arden родилась около 1514 г. в местности уилмакот, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas Arden of Wilmcote было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Andrews, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Webb в 1533 г. в местности стратфорд он авон, варвиккшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 3 декабря 1609 г. в местности биркшир, Англия в возрасте 96 лет и была похоронена в местности виндзор, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Grace Arden nació aproximadamente 1514, en Wilmcote, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Thomas Arden of Wilmcote, tenía 46 años y su mamá, Mary Andrews, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Sir William Webb en 1533, en Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 3 de diciembre de 1609, en Berkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 96 años, y fue sepultada en Windsor, Berkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
